home.subtitle: The Classic South Indian Board Game

settings.title: Settings
settings.mustCutBeforeInner: Must cut opponent before inner path

howToPlay.title: How to Play
howToPlay.boardText: Dhayam is played on a cross-shaped board with a track of 52 squares around the outside and 6 inner squares per player leading to the center Home square. Coloured zones at each corner are the starting areas.
howToPlay.diceText:
Four long sticks are used as dice. Each stick has two faces — scored and unscored. Throw all four and count how many land scored-side up. All unscored = 'Thayam' (special bonus!).

0 scored (Thayam) = move 5
1 scored = move 1
2 scored = move 2
3 scored = move 3
4 scored = move 8
howToPlay.movingText: Coins start off the board. Roll a 1 or 5 (Thayam) to enter. Once on the board coins move clockwise around the outer track then inward toward the center. A coin must land EXACTLY on the center to finish — no overshooting.
howToPlay.cuttingText: If you land on an opponent's coin on a non-safe square, their coin is sent back home! This is called a 'cut'. Safe squares (marked with a star ★) protect coins from being cut. Rolling 1, 5 or 8 earns an extra roll.
howToPlay.winningText: The first player to move all their coins into the center Home wins the game!

common.error: An error occurred. Please try again.
